aids.title: Reverse: S-R-M-M
aids.content:
**S**ave next, **R**everse link, **M**ove prev, **M**ove current.

next = curr->next; curr->next = prev; prev = curr; curr = next;

aids.title: Slow-Fast = Tortoise-Hare
aids.content: Like the fable! Slow tortoise moves 1 step, fast hare moves 2 steps. When hare finishes race (end of list), tortoise is at middle.

aids.title: Cycle Detection Logic
aids.content: If there's a cycle, fast will 'lap' slow. Like runners on a track - the faster one eventually catches up if track is circular.

aids.title: ESN - Edge Cases
aids.content: **E**mpty list (head == NULL), **S**ingle node (head->next == NULL), **N**th from end (n > length). Always check ESN!
aids.forRemembering: Common edge cases in linked list

aids.title: Head = O(1), Everywhere else = O(n)
aids.content: Operations at HEAD are O(1). Everything else requires traversal = O(n). This is the key trade-off of singly linked lists.
